headline: Disney’s Upcoming Movie Releases Should Be Good for the Stock, Analyst Says
summary: Walt Disney  stock could finally get the boost shareholders have long been hoping for, thanks in part to an exciting movie release schedule ahead, one analyst wrote on Friday.  Rosenblatt Securities analyst Barton Crockett raised his price target for  Disney  to $126 from $120, which implies a 27% increase from the stock’s last closing price of $99.34.  Crockett wrote in a research note that Disney’s movie slate “looks substantially more profitable in F2026 than F2025.”

Crockett wrote in a research note that Disney’s movie slate “looks substantially more profitable in F2026 than F2025.”

headline: Is Papa John’s (PZZA) Toy Story 5 Tie-Up Reinforcing Its Brand Moat or Just Buzz?
summary: Papa John’s International has already launched a global collaboration with Disney and Pixar’s Toy Story 5, featuring themed personal pizzas, collectibles, in-app games, and immersive “Pizza Planet” pop-ups across more than 40 markets for a limited time. This tie-up blends Papa John’s product innovation with one of Disney’s best-known family franchises, creating a broad marketing showcase for the brand’s quality-focused positioning and digital engagement tools. Next, we’ll examine how this...

headline: 50-year-old theme park to close forever after summer season
summary: Theme park giants like Disney and Universal can withstand periodic drops in visitor numbers, but a bad season can spell doom for a smaller theme park. After posting a loss of more than $1.2 billion at the end of 2025, Six Flags shared that it will be closing six "underperforming" locations in its ...

headline: Scoop: Smash leads $200M deal for defense-tech Allen Control Systems
summary: Years before he helped launch Disney+, or helped the Mouse buy Marvel and Star Wars, Kevin Mayer studied mechanical engineering at MIT and spent time working on battlefield radar systems and signal processors.

headline: Netflix vs. Disney: Which Streaming Stock Is the Better Long-Term Hold?
summary: For a retirement-focused investor choosing between Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) and The Walt Disney Company (NYSE:DIS), which streaming giant deserves a slot in the portfolio right now? Both have transformed since the streaming wars began, but they offer fundamentally different risk and reward profiles. Netflix is the lean, scaled growth machine. Disney is the diversified cash-return story ... Netflix vs. Disney: Which Streaming Stock Is the Better Long-Term Hold?
